techniques.evidence_quote: the 3rd party Amazon ECS task definition that the customer was using, was unintentionally exposing a web interface to the internet. That interface allowed unauthenticated users to run commands on the system.
That interface allowed unauthenticated users to run commands on the system.
the threat actor decided to leave with just the one Amazon ECS task running crypto mining
a number of attempts to use the credentials of the Task IAM role that was associated with the Amazon ECS task
failed with either a Client.UnauthorizedOperation or an AccessDenied error message
attempts to use the credentials of the Task IAM role that was associated with the Amazon ECS task
running a number of Amazon EC2 instances (most likely for surreptitious crypto mining)

attack_chain.description: Threat actor discovered and accessed a web interface unintentionally exposed to the internet by a third-party ECS task definition.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the 3rd party Amazon ECS task definition that the customer was using, was unintentionally exposing a web interface to the internet

attack_chain.description: Attacker used the exposed web interface to execute arbitrary commands on the ECS task container without authentication.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: That interface allowed unauthenticated users to run commands on the system.

evidence_chunks.quote: That interface allowed unauthenticated users to run commands on the system.
That interface allowed unauthenticated users to run commands on the system.

attack_chain.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
attack_chain.description: Attacker obtained the ECS Task IAM role credentials, likely via the container/instance metadata service.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: attempts to use the credentials of the Task IAM role that was associated with the Amazon ECS task

evidence_chunks.quote: attempts to use the credentials of the Task IAM role that was associated with the Amazon ECS task
attempts to use the credentials of the Task IAM role that was associated with the Amazon ECS task

attack_chain.description: Attacker attempted to leverage the stolen Task IAM role credentials to perform AWS API actions.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a number of attempts to use the credentials of the Task IAM role

evidence_chunks.quote: a number of attempts to use the credentials of the Task IAM role
a number of attempts to use the credentials of the Task IAM role

attack_chain.technique_name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Create Cloud Instance
attack_chain.description: Attacker attempted to launch multiple EC2 instances via RunInstances for crypto mining; all attempts were denied due to the Task IAM role only granting s3:* permissions.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The majority of actions were attempting to launch multiple Amazon EC2 instances via RunInstances calls. Every one of these actions ... failed with either a Client.UnauthorizedOperation or an AccessDenied error message.

attack_chain.description: Unable to launch additional EC2 instances, the attacker instead ran crypto mining within the compromised ECS task, triggering GuardDuty CryptoCurrency:EC2/BitcoinTool alerts.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the threat actor decided to leave with just the one Amazon ECS task running crypto mining

evidence_chunks.quote: the threat actor decided to leave with just the one Amazon ECS task running crypto mining
the threat actor decided to leave with just the one Amazon ECS task running crypto mining

initial_access_vector: Remote code execution via an unauthenticated web interface unintentionally exposed to the internet by a third-party Amazon ECS task definition.
persistence_mechanism: None established; attacker's RunInstances attempts to spawn additional EC2 instances were blocked by IAM policy limited to s3:*.
privilege_escalation: Attempted to use stolen ECS Task IAM role credentials to gain access to additional AWS resources (EC2), but the role only had s3:* permissions so escalation failed.
